'''Kane Bentley''' (born 16 April 1987) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hk}} for the [[St Gaudens Bears]] in the [[Elite One Championship]]. He has played for both [[France national rugby league team|France]] and [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] at international level.

He previously played for [[Toulouse Olympique Broncos]] in the [[Elite One Championship]].

==Early years==
Bentley was born in New Zealand, he has [[Scottish people|Scottish]] [[ancestor]]s, and eligible to play for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] due to the [[Grandfather rule|grandparent rule]].

Born to a New Zealand/Samoan father and New Zealand Maori mother in Auckland, Bentley moved to France at a young age where his father played for [[La Réole XIII]].

==Playing career==
His senior league started at Marseilles then UTC. In 2007, he gained promotion to the Dragons first team squad alongside his brother [[Andrew Bentley|Andrew]], and then moved to Toulouse. In 2015 he signed with the [[Boston 13s]] in the United States, helping them to the [[2015 USARL season|2015 USARL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.to13.com/kane-bentley-au-pays-de-loncle-sam/ |title=Kane BENTLEY au pays de l'Oncle Sam – Toulouse Olympique XIII |access-date=2015-06-24 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150624171320/http://www.to13.com/kane-bentley-au-pays-de-loncle-sam/ |archive-date=2015-06-24 }}</ref>

===Saint-Gaudens Bears===
On 3 June 2020 it was reported that he had signed for [[Saint-Gaudens Bears]] in the [[Elite One Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Trieze Mondial.fr|date=3 Jun 2020|access-date=11 Jan 2021|title=Saint-Gaudens signe Kane Bentley|url=https://treizemondial.fr/saint-gaudens-signe-kane-bentley/}}</ref>

==Representative career==
Bentley represented France at junior level for a number of years.

He was named in the [[France national rugby league team|France]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]], and for the [[2009 Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations tournament]].<ref name="France dominated by Dragons">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3949078,00.html|title=France dominated by Dragons|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2008-08-07|access-date=2008-08-08}}</ref> Bentley was also eligible for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] but he was snubbed for the [[2009 European Cup|European Cup]], and instead opted to play for France.<ref name="NZ_Herald_10606053">{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10606053&ref=rss|title=League: Kiwi hooker looking to make mark with France|author=Mascord, Steve|date=29 October 2009|work= [[The New Zealand Herald]]|access-date=8 October 2011}}</ref>

He has since been a regular for the senior side, and was named in the squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].
